features.summary: Alphabet delivered strong Q4 2024 results with revenue of $96.5 billion (up 12% YoY) and full-year revenue of $350 billion (up 14% YoY). Google Services grew 10% to $84.1 billion, driven by Search (up 13%) and YouTube ads (up 14%). Google Cloud accelerated to 30% growth reaching $12 billion quarterly revenue. The company achieved its goal of $110 billion combined annual run rate for YouTube and Cloud. Operating margin expanded significantly to 32%, and the company returned nearly $70 billion to shareholders in 2024. AI innovations including Gemini 2.0 and AI Overviews are driving user engagement and developer adoption.
features.management_outlook: Management expects 2025 to be a significant year for Search innovation with continued AI integration and agentic capabilities. They plan to invest approximately $75 billion in CapEx for 2025, primarily for technical infrastructure including servers and data centers. The company will focus on headcount growth in key areas like AI and Cloud while driving operational efficiencies. Management sees strong demand for AI products exceeding current capacity, requiring continued infrastructure investment throughout the year to meet customer needs.
features.risks: Q1 2025 faces headwinds from foreign exchange rates due to dollar strengthening and one less day of revenue compared to leap year Q1 2024. The company will lap strong financial services vertical performance from 2024, particularly in insurance. Cloud revenue growth may show variability depending on timing of new capacity deployment. Increasing CapEx investments will create pressure on P&L through higher depreciation, with depreciation growth expected to accelerate in 2025. The company currently has more AI product demand than available capacity.
features.key_drivers: AI infrastructure and model capabilities are driving growth across all segments, with Gemini 2.0 powering enhanced user experiences and developer adoption doubling to 4.4 million. Search benefits from AI Overviews expansion to 100+ countries and Circle to Search on 200+ million devices. Cloud growth is fueled by AI infrastructure demand, generative AI solutions, and strategic enterprise deals over $1 billion. YouTube maintains streaming leadership with record market share, strong Shorts monetization progress, and successful podcast integration. The full-stack AI approach provides competitive advantages in cost, performance, and latency optimization.
